scope.summary:
Goal: Implement code folding derived from the tree-sitter parse tree. Foldable regions are identified from tree-sitter node types (function bodies, class bodies, block statements, if/else branches, comment blocks). Folded regions are collapsed to a single summary line with an ellipsis. The gutter (rendered in MT-007) shows fold triangles; this MT only computes fold regions and applies them to the virtual line layout. Expected diff shape: new module `src/frontend/code_editor/folding.rs`, edits to `src/frontend/code_editor/mod.rs` and `src/frontend/panels/code_editor_panel.rs`.

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/code_editor/folding.rs` — defines `FoldRegion { start_line: usize, end_line: usize, folded: bool, label: String }` where `label` is the first line of the region plus `...` (e.g. `fn main() {…}`). Implements `FoldProvider::compute(tree: &tree_sitter::Tree, buffer: &TextBuffer) -> Vec<FoldRegion>` — walks the tree-sitter syntax tree using `tree.root_node().children()` recursively, identifies nodes whose type is in a configurable set (`FoldableNodeTypes`), and extracts their start/end row from `node.start_position().row` / `node.end_position().row`. Returns only regions spanning at least 2 lines. Also: `FoldSet { regions: Vec<FoldRegion> }` with methods `toggle(start_line: usize)`, `is_line_visible(line: usize) -> bool` (returns false if the line falls inside a folded region that is not the start line), `visible_line_to_buffer_line(visible_line: usize) -> usize` (maps from visible (post-fold) line index to actual buffer line index, accounting for collapsed regions).
- EDIT `src/frontend/code_editor/mod.rs` — add `fold_set: FoldSet` to `CodeEditorPanel`. After each buffer edit or tree-sitter re-parse, call `FoldProvider::compute` and update `fold_set`. In `show()`, wrap the `VirtualLineLayout` visible_range computation with `FoldSet::is_line_visible` filtering so folded lines are skipped.
- EDIT `src/frontend/panels/code_editor_panel.rs` — in the line rendering loop, when the current line is the start line of a folded region, render the summary label line (first line text + `…`) instead of the actual line, and skip all subsequent lines until `end_line`. The gutter click handler (for fold triangles, to be rendered in MT-007) calls `fold_set.toggle(start_line)`.

Foldable node types (initial set for Rust and JS):
- Rust: `function_item`, `impl_item`, `struct_item`, `enum_item`, `block`, `match_expression`, `use_declaration` (group).
- JS/TS: `function_declaration`, `arrow_function`, `class_declaration`, `block_statement`, `object`, `array`.
- Store in a `HashMap<&'static str, &'static [&'static str]>` keyed by language id.

Exact crates/functions/APIs to use:
- `tree-sitter` — already a dependency from MT-001. Use `Node::kind()`, `Node::start_position()`, `Node::end_position()`, `Node::children()` cursor API.
- No additional crates needed.
- AccessKit: add `author_id = "code_editor_fold_{start_line}"` `Role::TreeItem` nodes for each foldable region, with `actions: [Action::Expand, Action::Collapse]` depending on fold state.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Implement `FoldProvider::compute` using a tree-sitter `TreeCursor` traversal (more efficient than recursive children). Push a `FoldRegion` whenever `cursor.node().kind()` is in `FoldableNodeTypes` for the current language and `end_row - start_row >= 1`.
2. Sort `FoldSet::regions` by `start_line` ascending. `is_line_visible`: binary-search for the last region with `start_line <= line`; if found and `folded == true` and `line > region.start_line` and `line <= region.end_line`, return false.
3. `visible_line_to_buffer_line`: iterate regions in order, accumulate skipped lines (for each folded region before the visible line, add `region.end_line - region.start_line` to a running offset), return `visible_line + offset`.
4. In the rendering loop: iterate visible line indices from VirtualLineLayout, map each to buffer line via `visible_line_to_buffer_line`, render normally unless the buffer line is the start of a folded region, in which case render the fold label.
5. Keyboard shortcut for fold/unfold: Ctrl+Shift+[ folds the region at the cursor; Ctrl+Shift+] unfolds. Wire these in the code editor key handler (MT-010 will formalize; pre-wire here).
6. Total line count for VirtualLineLayout must account for folds: compute `visible_line_count` as `buffer.len_lines() - sum(folded_region.end_line - folded_region.start_line for all folded regions)` and pass this to `VirtualLineLayout::new`.

Dependencies/unblock conditions: MT-001 (tree-sitter parse tree available in CodeEditorPanel), MT-002 (VirtualLineLayout — folding modifies the effective line count that it receives).
Risk if missed: Large files (thousands of lines) are harder to navigate without folding. The gutter (MT-007) renders fold triangles and needs the fold region list.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend fold_provider` passes — tests: parse a 20-line Rust function, compute returns at least one FoldRegion covering the function body; single-line nodes produce no fold region.

AC-002: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend fold_set_visibility` passes — tests: folded region lines 5-10, is_line_visible(4)=true, is_line_visible(5)=true (start line visible), is_line_visible(6)=false, is_line_visible(10)=false, is_line_visible(11)=true.
AC-003: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend fold_set_mapping` passes — tests: visible_line_to_buffer_line with one folded region of 5 lines correctly offsets buffer lines after the region.
AC-004: egui_kittest screenshot with one region folded shows the fold label line (containing '…') and the subsequent lines of the region are absent.
AC-005: AccessKit tree contains a node with author_id='code_editor_fold_0' and role=TreeItem and action=Expand when that region is folded.
AC-006: Toggle fold twice returns to original unfolded state (idempotency test).
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend fold_provider` — GREEN, logged.
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend fold_set_visibility` — GREEN, logged.
PT-003: `cargo test -p handshake_frontend fold_set_mapping` — GREEN, logged.
PT-004: egui_kittest screenshot `test_output/MT-005-folded.png` pixel-verified for fold label presence and collapsed lines absence.
PT-005: egui_kittest AccessKit test `fold_accesskit` — fold_0 TreeItem node present.
implementation_notes: Use `tree_sitter::TreeCursor` (tree.walk()) instead of recursive function calls — avoids stack overflow on deeply nested trees.
Fold label: take `buffer.line(start_line).to_string().trim_end()` + ` …`. Truncate to 80 chars if the line is very long.
Do not recompute fold regions every frame — recompute only when `buffer_version` changes (same version counter used for highlight cache in MT-002).

Nested folds: a fold region can contain inner fold regions; `is_line_visible` must handle this — outer fold hides all inner folds and their lines. The outer fold toggle does not change inner fold state.
accessible fold state: when toggling fold, update the AccessKit action on the node (Expand <-> Collapse) so swarm agents can address it.
ports_from_react: Monaco handles folding internally in MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx (no explicit React code for folding). The native implementation uses tree-sitter node types as the fold source — equivalent to Monaco's built-in language-aware folding.
binds_backend_api: None — fold computation is purely in-process from the tree-sitter parse tree.

risks: RISK-001: visible_line_to_buffer_line is O(N folds) — on a file with 1000 folds this is called for every visible line on every frame. Mitigate: cache the mapping as a `Vec<usize>` indexed by visible line; invalidate when fold state changes.

RISK-002: Nested fold regions interact incorrectly — outer fold includes inner fold region lines in its `end_line`, but inner fold skips fewer lines than expected. Mitigate: in `compute`, merge overlapping regions by taking the outer bounds.

RISK-003: Fold regions computed from a stale tree after a fast edit — the tree-sitter parse lags the buffer by one frame. Mitigate: tolerate stale fold regions for one frame; do not crash if a fold region's start_line exceeds the current buffer length.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Cache fold mapping Vec — document the invalidation strategy in a code comment.
MC-002: Stale region guard: clamp start_line and end_line to buffer.len_lines() - 1 before use.
MC-003: Nested fold test included in AC-002 test suite.
